titel: Double penalties for crimes committed within criminal networks, and stricter sentencing guidelines.
html:
The government's proposal includes several measures aimed at creating a more just penal system, where punishments better reflect the severity of the crime, the perspective of victims is given greater weight, and societal security is improved. These proposals include the following:

  • Approximately fifty penalty ranges will be tightened.
  • A new regulation will be introduced for the common penalty scale in cases of multiple offenses, stipulating that the maximum sentence that can be imposed is double the highest maximum penalty.
  • A life sentence may be imposed as a common sentence for certain serious cases of repeated violent and sexual offenses.
  • A new aggravating factor will be introduced, applicable to all crimes linked to criminal networks. When this factor applies, the severity of the crime should initially be assessed as twice as high as it would otherwise be.
  • The presumption for pre-trial detention will be extended to include the crimes of aggravated domestic violence, aggravated harassment, and honor-based oppression. This presumption should also apply when the suspicion relates to an attempt, preparation, or incitement to a crime for which a penalty less severe than imprisonment for four years is not prescribed.

The proposed amendments to the law will come into effect on August 1, 2026.

Proposal for a Parliamentary Decision

Government Proposal:

  1. The Parliament adopts the government's proposed law amending the Penal Code.
  2. The Parliament adopts the government's proposed law amending the Code of Criminal Procedure.
  3. The Parliament adopts the government's proposed law amending the Law on Tax Crimes (1971:69).
  4. The Parliament adopts the government's proposed law amending the Law (2000:1225) on penalties for smuggling.
  5. The Parliament adopts the government's proposed law amending the Law (2007:612) on crimes related to benefits.
  6. The Parliament adopts the government's proposed law amending the Law (2014:307) on penalties for money laundering offenses.
  7. The Parliament adopts the government's proposed law amending the Law (2016:1307) on penalties for market abuse in the securities market.
  8. The Parliament adopts the government's proposed law amending the Law (2025:1267) on crimes related to subsidies.

6

If an offense referred to in section 5 is considered aggravated, a conviction for aggravated assault carries a prison sentence of at least one year and six months, and at most seven years. In assessing whether the offense is aggravated, particular consideration should be given to whether the act was life-threatening, or whether the perpetrator has caused a serious bodily injury or serious illness, or otherwise shown particular disregard or brutality.

If the offense is considered particularly aggravated, a conviction for particularly aggravated assault carries a prison sentence of at least six and at most twelve years. In assessing whether the offense is particularly aggravated, particular consideration should be given to whether the bodily injury is permanent, or whether the act has caused particularly severe suffering, or whether the perpetrator has shown particular disregard.
tldr: The government proposes stricter penalties for crimes committed by criminal organizations and for several other types of offenses. The proposal suggests that penalties could be doubled to better protect society.
lattlast:
The government has proposed new laws that are scheduled to come into effect on August 1, 2026. The most important aspect of the proposal is that the penalties for many different crimes will be increased. If a crime is linked to criminal networks, the government proposes that the penalty should be doubled compared to the usual penalty.

There are also new rules for individuals who commit multiple crimes simultaneously. If someone commits several crimes, the maximum penalty could be doubled compared to the usual penalty for each individual crime. For certain very serious cases of repeated violent crimes and sexual offenses, the government proposes that individuals could be sentenced to life imprisonment.

The proposal also changes the rules regarding when a person can be held in pre-trial detention, meaning they can be held in custody while awaiting trial. This will now be easier for crimes involving serious violations of domestic tranquility and honor crimes. The government also wants to change laws related to everything from smuggling and tax fraud to money laundering and welfare fraud.

The text also describes specific rules for assault. If an assault is considered serious, the penalty can range from one year and six months to seven years in prison. If the assault is considered particularly serious, meaning it is extremely serious or callous, the penalty can range from six to twelve years in prison.
